senses.definition: formally ended or cancelled a law, rule, or agreement
examples: The outdated law was finally abrogated by parliament.
The treaty was abrogated after years of political tension.

senses.definition: failed to do something that one is responsible for
examples: The company abrogated its duty to protect its workers.
They abrogated their responsibilities to the community.

senses.definition: formally ends or cancels a law, rule, or agreement
examples: The new act abrogates the previous regulations.
This clause abrogates all earlier contracts on the matter.

senses.definition: fails to do something that one is responsible for
examples: The state abrogates its responsibility when it cuts essential services.
He abrogates his duty as a leader if he stays silent.

senses.definition: formally ending or cancelling a law, rule, or agreement
examples: By abrogating the treaty, the government risked international criticism.
They are abrogating old regulations that no longer make sense.

senses.definition: failing to do something that one is responsible for
examples: By ignoring the warning signs, the board was abrogating its duty of care.
He is abrogating his responsibilities as a parent.

senses.definition: the formal ending or cancelling of a law, rule, or agreement
examples: The abrogation of the treaty caused tension between the two countries.
Many citizens protested the sudden abrogation of their rights.

senses.definition: acts of formally ending or cancelling laws, rules, or agreements
examples: The constitution lists the conditions for abrogations of emergency powers.
Repeated abrogations of civil liberties led to public unrest.

senses.definition: having the effect of ending or cancelling a law or rule
examples: The court issued an abrogative judgment on the controversial statute.
This abrogative clause nullifies all previous agreements.

senses.definition: a person or authority that formally ends or cancels a law, rule, or agreement
examples: The legislature is the main abrogator of outdated regulations.
In this case, the president acted as the abrogator of the treaty.

senses.definition: people or authorities that formally end or cancel laws, rules, or agreements
examples: They were seen as abrogators of hard-won civil liberties.
Courts and parliaments can both be abrogators of existing rules.

senses.definition: a genus of tropical Asian shrubs or small trees, including a species used for its fiber
examples: Abroma plants grow in warm, humid regions.
The bark of Abroma augusta is sometimes used in traditional medicine.

senses.definition: a genus of flowering plants in the four o'clock family, native to North and Central America
examples: Abronia species are sometimes called sand verbenas.
The bright flowers of abronia attract many pollinators.

senses.definition: over or upon the nest, especially while sitting on eggs (archaic or poetic)
examples: The hen sat abrood for many days.
The bird remained abrood in the early morning light.

senses.definition: endure or tolerate something (archaic)
examples: He would not abrook such an insult.
She could no longer abrook his rude behavior.

senses.definition: a past tense form of abrook, meaning endured or tolerated (archaic)
examples: He abrooke many hardships in silence.
They abrooke the long winter without complaint.

senses.definition: endured or tolerated something (archaic past form)
examples: She never abrooked any sign of disloyalty.
They abrooked the harsh conditions for years.

senses.definition: endures or tolerates something (archaic)
examples: He abrookes no criticism of his work.
The king abrookes no rivals to his power.

senses.definition: enduring or tolerating something (archaic)
examples: He was abrooking no delay in the matter.
They marched on, abrooking every hardship.

senses.definition: the refusal or inability to eat, especially as a symptom of illness (rare)
examples: The patient showed signs of abrosia after the operation.
Long-term abrosia can lead to serious health problems.

senses.definition: instances or cases of refusal or inability to eat (rare)
examples: The doctor reported several abrosias among the elderly patients.
Untreated abrosias may require hospital care.

senses.definition: a plant also known as southernwood, used in traditional medicine and gardening
examples: Abrotanum has a strong scent and fine leaves.
Some herbalists still use abrotanum in their remedies.

senses.definition: a bitter substance obtained from the plant abrotanum (rare, technical)
examples: Researchers isolated abrotin from the dried leaves.
Abrotin was once studied for its possible medicinal effects.

senses.definition: a chemical compound related to the plant abrotanum (rare, technical)
examples: Abrotine was detected during analysis of the plant extract.
The pharmacological effects of abrotine are not well understood.

senses.definition: sudden and unexpected, often in an unpleasant way
examples: There was an abrupt change in the weather.
The meeting came to an abrupt end.

senses.definition: speaking or behaving in a way that seems rude because it is very direct and quick
examples: His manner was so abrupt that people found him unfriendly.
She gave me an abrupt answer and walked away.

senses.definition: very steep or almost vertical
examples: They climbed an abrupt cliff face.
The path ended at an abrupt drop into the sea.

senses.definition: in a very sudden or broken way (rare, nonstandard)
examples: The conversation ended abruptedly.
The road dropped off abruptedly at the cliff.

senses.definition: more sudden or more direct and possibly rude
examples: His tone grew even abrupter as the interview went on.
The storm’s arrival was abrupter than anyone expected.

senses.definition: most sudden or most direct and possibly rude
examples: It was the abruptest ending to a film I had ever seen.
Her reply was the abruptest of all the comments.

senses.definition: a medical term meaning a tearing away, especially of the placenta from the uterus before birth
examples: Abruptio placentae can cause heavy bleeding in the mother.
Doctors treated the patient for abruptio during late pregnancy.

senses.definition: a sudden breaking away or separation, especially of the placenta from the uterus before birth
examples: Placental abruption is a serious complication of pregnancy.
The doctor explained the symptoms of abruption to the family.

senses.definition: multiple abruptions, especially placental separations (rare, technical plural)
examples: The study reported several abruptiones during the final trimester.
Abruptiones are rare but dangerous events in pregnancy.

senses.definition: sudden separations, especially of the placenta from the uterus before birth
examples: Placental abruptions require urgent medical care.
The hospital recorded two abruptions that month.

senses.definition: suddenly and unexpectedly, often in an unpleasant way
examples: The car stopped abruptly in the middle of the road.
The interview ended abruptly after his comment.

senses.definition: in a direct and sometimes rude way
examples: She spoke so abruptly that I felt unwelcome.
He turned away abruptly without saying goodbye.

senses.definition: the quality of being sudden and unexpected
examples: The abruptness of the announcement shocked everyone.
He apologized for the abruptness of the ending.

senses.definition: the quality of being very direct and possibly rude
examples: Her abruptness often offended people.
He tried to soften the abruptness of his tone.

senses.definition: different kinds or instances of abrupt quality, in change or manner
examples: Their various abruptnesses made the conversation difficult.
The writer describes the abruptnesses of life in a big city.

senses.definition: sudden or steep places or breaks (rare, literary plural)
examples: The path wound along cliffs and other abrupts.
They avoided the sharp abrupts of the mountain trail.

senses.definition: a genus of tropical climbing plants, including a species with very poisonous seeds
examples: Abrus precatorius has bright red seeds.
Children should be warned not to handle abrus seeds.

senses.definition: the muscles on the front of your stomach
examples: He does sit-ups every day to strengthen his abs.
Her abs are very toned from regular exercise.

senses.definition: short for abdominal exercises
examples: The workout includes five minutes of abs.
We finished with some abs and stretching.

senses.definition: short for anti-lock braking system in vehicles
examples: Modern cars are usually equipped with ABS.
The ABS helped prevent the wheels from locking.

senses.definition: a male given name, especially in biblical and literary contexts, variant of Absalom
examples: Absalom is a name found in the Old Testament.
The character was called Absalom, after the biblical figure.

senses.definition: a former unit of electric current in the centimeter–gram–second system, equal to ten amperes
examples: The absampere is now rarely used in modern physics.
Early textbooks sometimes refer to current in absamperes.

senses.definition: a mountain range and region in the United States, also the name of a Native American group
examples: They went hiking in the Absaroka Range.
The history of the Absaroka people is complex.

senses.definition: a type of volcanic rock found in the Absaroka region
examples: Geologists collected samples of absarokite from the site.
Absarokite is known for its specific mineral composition.

senses.definition: the name of a famous FBI operation in the late 1970s and early 1980s that investigated political corruption in the United States
examples: Abscam led to the conviction of several members of Congress.
The film was loosely based on the Abscam scandal.

senses.definition: a swollen, painful area in the body that is filled with pus
examples: She had a painful abscess on her gum.
The doctor drained the abscess on his leg.

senses.definition: swollen and filled with pus because of infection
examples: He went to the dentist about an abscessed tooth.
The vet treated the dog's abscessed paw.

senses.definition: became swollen and filled with pus
examples: The wound abscessed after a few days.
If not cleaned properly, the cut may abscess.

senses.definition: swollen, painful areas in the body that are filled with pus
examples: Skin abscesses can be very painful.
The clinic treats many dental abscesses every day.

senses.definition: becoming swollen and filled with pus
examples: The cut was abscessing and needed treatment.
If the area starts abscessing, see a doctor.

senses.definition: the separation or breaking off of a part, especially the falling off of leaves or fruit from a plant
examples: Leaf abscession occurs in autumn.
Hormones play a role in fruit abscession.

senses.definition: a North American plant used in traditional medicine to treat abscesses and other skin problems
examples: Herbalists sometimes use abscessroot in their remedies.
Abscessroot was boiled and applied to the skin.

senses.definition: cut off or remove something by cutting (formal, rare)
examples: The surgeon had to abscind the damaged tissue.
They decided to abscind the last paragraph from the report.

senses.definition: cut off or removed something by cutting (formal, rare past form)
examples: The surgeon abscinded the growth successfully.
They abscinded the final section of the document.

senses.definition: cutting off or removing something by cutting (formal, rare)
examples: The procedure involves abscinding the damaged area.
Abscinding the extra text made the article clearer.

senses.definition: cuts off or removes something by cutting (formal, rare)
examples: The process abscinds the dead tissue from the healthy skin.
This rule abscinds the last part of the earlier law.
